basics.name: Dr. C. Tufan Cakir
basics.summary: PhD-trained Applied AI Engineer and Data Scientist with 7+ years of experience in machine learning, statistical modelling, Python-based analytical systems, and workflow automation in complex technical environments. Strong background in optimisation under uncertainty, adaptive data acquisition, decision-support tools, and translating research ideas into robust, reusable, and deployable technical solutions. Experienced in working across interdisciplinary teams and building data-driven systems for real-world experimental and operational settings.

work.name: Bundesanstalt für Materialforschung und -prüfung (BAM)
work.summary: Designed and implemented machine-learning-based evaluation workflows, digital twins, Python-based analytical software, and decision-support tools for complex experimental and technical systems.
highlights: Designed and implemented machine-learning-based evaluation workflows and digital twins for complex experimental systems.
Developed production-oriented Python software for real-time data acquisition, automated analysis, and decision support in complex technical workflows.
Applied Bayesian optimisation with Gaussian process models to explore a four-dimensional parameter space efficiently, replacing random or exhaustive search strategies.
Built robust and reusable analytical pipelines in collaboration with interdisciplinary teams in research and technical environments.

work.name: Bundesanstalt für Materialforschung und -prüfung (BAM)
work.summary: Developed Python-based data analysis pipelines and machine-learning workflows for spectroscopy and materials-related studies under real laboratory conditions.
highlights: Developed Python-based data analysis pipelines with explicit uncertainty estimation for spectroscopy and materials-related studies.
Applied machine learning to predict experimental outcomes, detect anomalies in high-dimensional data, and improve measurement efficiency.
Worked with automated measurement systems and large-scale datasets under real laboratory conditions.

courses: Thesis: Optimisation of depth-resolved X-ray absorption spectroscopy in grazing emission mode for complex alloys

publications.name: Machine learning for efficient grazing-exit X-ray absorption near edge structure spectroscopy analysis: Bayesian optimization approach
publications.summary: A machine-learning-based approach using Bayesian optimisation for efficient GE-XANES analysis and adaptive experimental design.

publications.name: Exploring the Nb2O5 coating deposited on the Ti-6Al-4V alloy by a novel GE-XANES technique and nanoindentation load-depth
publications.summary: Application of GE-XANES and nanoindentation methods to investigate Nb2O5 coatings on Ti-6Al-4V alloy.

publications.name: BAMline — A real-life sample materials research beamline
publications.summary: Description of BAMline as a materials research beamline for real-life sample investigations.

publications.name: Exploring the Depths of Corrosion: A Novel GE-XANES Technique for Investigating Compositionally Complex Alloys
publications.summary: Development and application of a GE-XANES method for depth-resolved investigation of corrosion in compositionally complex alloys.

publications.name: Time-, space- and energy-resolved in situ characterization of catalysts by X-ray absorption spectroscopy
publications.summary: In situ X-ray absorption spectroscopy study with time-, space-, and energy-resolved characterization of catalysts.

publications.name: Dispersive X-ray absorption spectroscopy for time-resolved in situ monitoring of mechanochemical reactions
publications.summary: Use of dispersive X-ray absorption spectroscopy for time-resolved in situ monitoring of mechanochemical reactions.

portfolio.name: SpecTwin: Digital Twin for Experimental Optimisation
portfolio.description: Python-based analytical software for automated spectrometer configuration, data processing, visualization, simulation workflow automation, digital twin based experimental planning, optimisation experiments, and decision-support logic.

portfolio.name: Conversational AI for Scientific Workflow Control
portfolio.description: Domain-specific AI assistant prototype that translates natural-language instructions into controlled workflow actions in a safety-aware experimental environment.

portfolio.name: Bayesian Active Learning for Resource-Efficient Data Acquisition
portfolio.description: Adaptive sampling workflow using Gaussian processes to reduce runtime from 20 hours to 25 minutes while balancing exploration and exploitation.

portfolio.name: Real-Time Decision Support for Automated Measurement Systems
portfolio.description: Python workflows for beamline alignment and on-the-fly data acquisition, combining modelling, optimisation, and modular software design.

portfolio.name: Modular Data Analysis Ecosystem
portfolio.description: Reusable Python toolkit for preprocessing, feature extraction, visualization, and evaluation to improve scalability and reproducibility across scientific data analysis tasks.
